transformation_type: diazonium coupling of 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to arylhydrazones
mechanism_type: azo coupling followed by rearrangement, decarboxylation, and hydrazone formation
common_conditions: prepare an arenediazonium salt and couple it with a beta-keto ester or other 1,3-dicarbonyl compound under buffered or basic conditions to obtain an arylhydrazone after rearrangement
description_short: Diazonium-coupling manifold that converts 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds into arylhydrazones, often as precursors to Fischer indole synthesis.

transformation_type: thermal Claisen rearrangement of allylic alcohols to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters
mechanism_type: allyl orthoester exchange and ketene-acetal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
common_conditions: heat an allylic alcohol with an excess trialkyl orthoacetate under weakly acidic conditions to form a ketene acetal that rearranges to a gamma,delta-unsaturated ester
description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement of allylic alcohols and ortho esters that installs gamma,delta-unsaturated ester motifs with predictable alkene geometry.

transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
mechanism_type: chromate ester formation followed by beta-hydrogen transfer/elimination
common_conditions: treat an alcohol with chromium trioxide or dichromate in acidic medium or with related chromium(VI) reagents to oxidize primary or secondary alcohols to aldehydes, ketones, or carboxylic acids
synonym_names: Jones Reagent Oxidation|Oxidation of Alcohols by Chromium Reagents|Sarett Oxidation|Collins Oxidation|PCC Oxidation|PDC Oxidation
description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

mechanism_type: addition of metallated sulfones to carbonyls followed by acylation and reductive fragmentation or Smiles-type elimination
common_conditions: add an alpha-metallated sulfone to an aldehyde or ketone, convert the beta-alkoxysulfone into a fragmentation precursor, and induce elimination to deliver an alkene, typically with E-selectivity
description_short: Classical and modified sulfone olefination strategies that transform carbonyl compounds into stereodefined alkenes, often favoring E geometry.

transformation_type: SmI2-mediated coupling of alkyl halides or halo carbonyl derivatives with carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: single-electron reduction to organosamarium or ketyl species followed by Barbier/Grignard-type addition, acyl substitution, or tandem cyclization
common_conditions: treat alkyl halides, iodides, or halo carbonyl precursors with samarium diiodide, often with HMPA or metal-salt additives, to couple them with aldehydes, ketones, or esters in inter- or intramolecular manifolds
description_short: Samarium diiodide-based coupling platform that enables intermolecular and intramolecular Barbier, Grignard, Reformatsky, and acyl-substitution processes in complex synthesis.
